news.title: Ahmedabad: Three construction sites in Gota sealed for not installing green nets
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation has sealed three major construction sites in Gota for failing to install mandatory green nets and barricades. Additionally, a ₹1 lakh fine was imposed on one site for material spillage onto public roads, while other sites faced penalties for pollution and safety violations. Encroachments were also cleared to improve traffic flow.

news.title: Residents of Secunderabad Cantonment Board seek reduction in transfer of property tax to 1.5%
news.para: Secunderabad Cantonment residents are protesting a 5% property tax rate set in 1989, arguing it's outdated and lacks public consultation. They demand a fresh notification, transparent objection process, and a reduction to 1.5%, aligning with GHMC rates. The Cantonment Vikas Manch highlights the need for fairness and democratic principles in tax imposition.

news.title: Retail leasing dips to 1.95 million sq ft in Q1 2026 amid supply constraints: Report
news.para: Retail leasing across India’s top cities declined in Q1 2026 due to lack of new supply, even as demand remained steady. Malls gained share amid rising preference for organised retail, while Delhi-NCR, Hyderabad and Mumbai led leasing activity.

news.title: Kalaburagi district posts record 89.8% property tax recovery
news.para: Kalaburagi district achieved a historic 89.8% property tax recovery, its highest in nine years, collecting Rs 33.4 crore by March 31, 2026. Strict measures by the zilla panchayat CEO, including doorstep collections and action against non-compliant officials, significantly boosted revenue, strengthening village panchayats financially.

news.title: KREEVA to invest ₹350-400 crore to develop residential project in Gurugram
news.para: Kreeva, Kanodia Group's real estate division, will invest ₹350-400 crore in a Gurugram housing project. The development will span three acres and offer around 200 residences. This project marks the company's entry into multi-generational housing. Construction is expected to begin by the end of the first quarter of fiscal year 2027.

news.title: Bhubaneswar civic body to survey residential buildings misused for commercial purposes
news.para: The Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation will conduct a comprehensive survey to identify residential buildings misused for commercial purposes, following a Supreme Court directive. Ward-level teams will inspect properties operating as offices, shops, or clinics without permission. This action addresses long-standing resident complaints about traffic, pollution, and infrastructure strain.

news.title: Bombay HC upholds ₹1 lakh payout for razing of illegal tenement without notice
news.para: Bombay High Court upheld a human rights commission order, directing Mhada to pay Rs 1 lakh compensation to a Jogeshwari resident. The court ruled that demolishing an unauthorized structure without notice violates the constitutional right to shelter, emphasizing due process even for encroachers.

news.title: Malkapur issues TDR certificates for acquiring land for public infrastructure
news.para: Malkapur Municipal Council in Satara district has become the first C-class urban civic body in Maharashtra to issue Transfer Development Right (TDR) certificates. Six landowners received certificates for approximately 19,000 square feet of land, facilitating acquisition for public infrastructure like Development Plan roads without direct monetary expenditure.

news.title: Lodha Developers reports ₹5,890 crore pre-sales in Q4 FY26
news.para: Lodha Developers reported a 23% year-on-year increase in pre-sales to ₹5,890 crore for the January-March quarter of FY26. Full-year pre-sales reached ₹20,530 crore, a 16% rise. The company also saw collections grow 18% year-on-year to ₹5,230 crore in the quarter and reduced net debt by ₹800 crore.

news.title: Lucknow development body halts demolition drive in Chinhat after resistance from builders
news.para: A Lucknow Development Authority demolition drive against illegal plotting in Chinhat was halted midway on Monday due to stiff resistance from builders and their associates. The LDA team faced stone-pelting and disruption near Kisan Path, forcing a withdrawal. The drive will be resumed with police protection.

news.title: Odisha registered 7,037 flats in last three years: Revenue minister
news.para: Odisha registered 7,037 flats in three years, boosting buyer ownership and land records. While 2024 saw a surge with 4,712 registrations, 2025 experienced a sharp decline to 593. Stricter scrutiny, enforcement of apartment laws, and action against erring officials contributed to the dip, with Khurda leading registrations.

news.title: Indore: 100 commercial buildings get final 48-hour ultimatum over fire safety
news.para: Indore authorities are intensifying a crackdown on fire safety violations, targeting around 100 commercial buildings. Building owners have until April 8 to install mandatory fire safety equipment or face immediate sealing and punitive action. This stern warning follows a survey revealing significant gaps in fire safety measures across the city.

news.title: Lucknow development body's three luxury housing projects to take off soon
news.para: Construction of three luxury housing projects by the Lucknow Development Authority is set to begin soon. Work orders are expected within a month. These projects will offer around 1,060 flats with prices ranging from Rs 70 lakh to Rs 2.80 crore. Registration is open for some schemes, with others nearing completion of allotment.

news.title: Land acquisition for New Nagpur project may cross ₹4,000 crore
news.para: The New Nagpur Business District project is facing increased land acquisition costs, now projected to surpass Rs4,000 crore. Farmers will soon receive final compensation details. This ambitious project, spanning over 692 hectares, aims to transform Nagpur into a business and finance hub. It is expected to create over five lakh jobs and features advanced infrastructure.

news.title: Layout approvals map Bengaluru's shifting growth
news.para: Bengaluru's urban expansion, tracked by BDA layout approvals since 1972, initially centered in the south. By the 1980s, growth shifted north, then eastward post-2000, driven by IT jobs and infrastructure. Residential development dominates, with recent trends showing denser construction and layout revisions, reflecting evolving demand and planning.

news.title: BMC plans amnesty scheme to grant OC to pre-Nov 2016 buildings in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai's BMC is launching an amnesty scheme to grant occupation certificates to older, occupied buildings lacking mandatory approval. The initiative, targeting structures built before November 2016 with minor deviations, offers a 50% concession on charges to resolve long-standing issues for residents and will be presented for approval soon.

news.title: Telangana: TGIIC to auction Osman Nagar land parcels on April 23
news.para: Telangana State Industrial Infrastructure Corporation (TSIIC) will auction two land parcels, a 10-acre and a 5.72-acre plot, in Osman Nagar on April 23. The upset price is fixed at ₹39 crore per acre, with a minimum bid increment of ₹25 lakh. This auction follows the deferral of an earlier sale due to market and procedural concerns.

news.title: Process for granting ownership rights in unauthorise colonies likely to transfer to Delhi government
news.para: Ownership rights for houses in Delhi's unauthorised colonies may soon shift from DDA to the Delhi government. This move aims to simplify the process for residents. The urban development department will handle regularisation. Municipal Corporation of Delhi will check building violations. This change could also see elected representatives playing a larger role in expediting property registrations.

news.title: Noida transforms into a luxury realty hub as corporates troop in
news.para: Noida is rapidly becoming a luxury real estate hub, challenging Gurugram's dominance. Developers are partnering with hospitality firms for high-end residences, with prices reaching ₹40 crore. The Noida International Airport is a key driver for this transformation. Affluent households are drawn to Noida's master-planned environments and branded spaces.

news.title: Aristo Pharma buys office space for ₹111 crore in Mumbai's Andheri West
news.para: Aristo Pharmaceuticals has acquired over 18,300 sq ft of office space across two floors in Andheri West's Parinee I for over Rs 111.26 crore. This significant outright purchase, registered on March 27, highlights sustained demand for premium commercial assets and a trend of end-users investing in owned office spaces for long-term commitment.
